page.content: In the period from 2019 to 2024, PetroChina's pipeline transportation tariffs show a consistent increase, starting from SGD1.50 in 2019, rising to SGD1.55 in 2020, SGD1.60 in 2021, SGD1.70 in 2022, SGD1.75 in 2023, and reaching SGD1.80 in 2024, with an average tariff of SGD1.70 across the total volume transported of 32,000,000 m³, generating total revenue of SGD54.67M against operational costs of SGD29.2M and a net profit of SGD25.47M, resulting in an average profit margin of 24%.

page.content: The volume transported increased from 5,000,000 m³ in 2019 to 6,000,000 m³ in 2024.

lines.text: The volume transported increased from 5,000,000 m³ in 2019 to 6,000,000 m³ in 2024.

page.content: The expenditure overview for upstream capital expenditure illustrates domestic projects amounting to $1,500M in 2019, increasing to $1,600M in 2020 with a year-on-year change of 6.67%, and further growing to $1,650M in 2021, $1,700M in 2022, $1,750M in 2023, and projected at $1,800M in 2024, holding a market share of 32% in 2019, 31% in 2020, 29% in 2021, with total expenditures reaching $2,400M in 2023 and projected to be $2,600M in 2024, reflecting a return on investment of 52% in 2023 and 51% in 2024. Foreign investments also saw growth from $700M in 2019 to $800M in 2020 with a year-on-year change of 14.29%, increasing to $850M in 2021 and $900M in 2024, while maintaining a market share of 20%, 22%, 21%, and 23% respectively throughout the years.
